title: Full Transcript: Bloom Energy Q1 2026 Earnings Call
text: The Q1 2026 Bloom Energy earnings call transcript reveals detailed management perspectives on operational execution, market dynamics, and strategic positioning. CEO KR Sridhar emphasized company's transformation into critical AI infrastructure provider. The company delivered $751.1 million revenue, representing 130.4% year-over-year growth, with non-GAAP EPS of $0.44 substantially exceeding consensus. Management highlighted that Bloom is rapidly becoming the standard and go-to choice for on-site power in AI data center markets. Product revenue surge of 208% to $653.3 million reflects unprecedented demand from hyperscalers building AI infrastructure. The company noted that AI data centers require immediate reliable power solutions that traditional grid infrastructure cannot provide within required timeframes. Bloom's ability to deploy fully operational systems in 90 days versus 4-8 year traditional infrastructure timelines creates competitive moat. Management raised full-year 2026 guidance significantly to $3.4-$3.8 billion revenue and $1.85-$2.25 non-GAAP EPS. The $20 billion backlog provides multi-year revenue visibility. CEO emphasized mission-driven culture and disciplined execution strategy. Management discussed manufacturing capacity expansion supporting hundreds of megawatts quarterly throughput increases.
